paper_title: Seeing Far and Clearly: Mitigating Hallucinations in MLLMs with Attention Causal Decoding
pope_metric_note: FarSight reports POPE accuracy (not F1). From Table 2 (image benchmarks): POPE-R=90.5 (+3.5 vs baseline 87.0); POPE-P=86.1 (+3.3 vs baseline 82.8); POPE-A=80.4 (+3.8 vs baseline 76.6). Confirmed in both Table 2 and the RoPE comparison table.

paper_title: Mitigating Visual Hallucinations via Semantic Curriculum Preference Optimization in MLLMs
pope_metric_note: SCPO does NOT report POPE scores. Uses AMBER-Discriminative F1 instead. Table 1 (vs larger models): '+SCPO-7B (ours) | 7.0(↓6.6) | 4.4(↓2.9) | ... | 85.4(↑2.0) | 89.2(↑1.8) |'. Table 2 (LLaVA-v1.6-7B + Hard): '+Hard(ours) | 7.0(↓5.0) | 4.4(↓2.37) | ... | 89.2(↑2.2) |'. Best AMBER-Disc F1=89.2 (v1.6-7B) and 87.3 (v1.5-7B).
chair_s_note: Table 1 (v1.6-7B, best model): '+SCPO-7B (ours) | 7.0(↓6.6)...' vs baseline LLaVA-v1.6-7B CHAIRS=12.0. Table 2 (v1.5-7B, +Hard): '+Hard(ours) | 20.3(↓34.4)...' vs baseline CHAIRS=54.7 (−62.9% = the '62.9%' reduction claimed in abstract). CHAIRS evaluated on Object HalBench (300 COCO val instances).
qwen3vl_results_reported: NO — only evaluated on LLaVA family (v1.5-7B / v1.6-7B / v1.5-13B); no Qwen3-VL experiments reported
